description:
You are a data-engineering assistant who has just received a small batch of raw click-stream events.  
Your goal is to organise the raw material in a new ETL workspace, perform a very light “T-step” (transform) and write a processing log that the automated grader can unambiguously validate.

Initial material already on disk  
/home/user/raw_data/raw_events_20240315.jsonl  ← a JSON-Lines file (one JSON object per line).

Requested end-state
1. A new workspace directory tree must exist exactly as shown:
   /home/user/etl/  
   ├── raw/  
   ├── processed/  
   └── logs/

2. The file raw_events_20240315.jsonl must live inside /home/user/etl/raw/ (it may no longer be present in /home/user/raw_data/).

3. From that raw file create the CSV file
   /home/user/etl/processed/events_20240315.csv  
   It must have precisely five lines (one header + four data rows) and use Unix line-endings (\n).  
   Exact required CSV content-order:
   ------------------------------------------------
   event_id,user_id,ts
   1001,55,2024-05-28T12:34:56Z
   1002,56,2024-05-28T12:35:56Z
   1003,57,2024-05-28T12:36:56Z
   1004,58,2024-05-28T12:37:56Z
   ------------------------------------------------

4. Write a processing log entry to  
   /home/user/etl/logs/processing_20240315.log  
   The file must contain exactly ONE line and be newline-terminated.  
   Format (all literal text except the timestamp which you generate at runtime):
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS|FILE=events_20240315.csv|LINES=4|STATUS=SUCCESS
   • Timestamp must be in 24-hour local time as produced by the command:  
     date "+%F %T"  
   • There must be no extra spaces, tabs, or surrounding quotes.  
   • For example (your timestamp will differ):  
     2024-06-01 09:57:03|FILE=events_20240315.csv|LINES=4|STATUS=SUCCESS

5. The original /home/user/raw_data/ directory should be left in place, but it must no longer contain raw_events_20240315.jsonl.

Complexity guideline  
The task can be completed with roughly 5-10 shell commands (mkdir, mv, cut/jq/awk, echo/printf, etc.).  Choose any standard GNU/Linux utilities; do NOT install external software.

Important grading rules  
• Path names, file names, capitalisation and file contents must match exactly.  
• The CSV file must contain **exactly** the lines shown above.  
• The log file must match the pattern  
  ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\|FILE=events_20240315\.csv\|LINES=4\|STATUS=SUCCESS\n$
• Do not print anything to standard output that changes the filesystem state; a normal amount of console output is fine.

Once you believe all five requirements are satisfied, you are done.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem items (present before the agent starts):

1. Directory:
/home/user/raw_data  (permissions 755, owned by user)

2. File:
/home/user/raw_data/raw_events_20240315.jsonl  
Exact 4-line content (each line ends with '\n'):
{"event_id": 1001, "user_id": 55, "ts": "2024-05-28T12:34:56Z", "meta": "foo"}
{"event_id": 1002, "user_id": 56, "ts": "2024-05-28T12:35:56Z", "meta": "bar"}
{"event_id": 1003, "user_id": 57, "ts": "2024-05-28T12:36:56Z", "meta": "baz"}
{"event_id": 1004, "user_id": 58, "ts": "2024-05-28T12:37:56Z", "meta": "bat"}

Expected final filesystem state after correct completion:

A. Directory tree:
/home/user/etl/              (755)
/home/user/etl/raw/          (755)
/home/user/etl/processed/    (755)
/home/user/etl/logs/         (755)

B. File now located in raw:
/home/user/etl/raw/raw_events_20240315.jsonl  
Content identical (bit-for-bit) to the original file listed above.

C. CSV produced:
/home/user/etl/processed/events_20240315.csv  
Content (5 lines, newline at end of each):
event_id,user_id,ts
1001,55,2024-05-28T12:34:56Z
1002,56,2024-05-28T12:35:56Z
1003,57,2024-05-28T12:36:56Z
1004,58,2024-05-28T12:37:56Z

D. Log file:
/home/user/etl/logs/processing_20240315.log  
Exactly one line, newline-terminated, matching regex  
^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\|FILE=events_20240315\.csv\|LINES=4\|STATUS=SUCCESS\n$

E. Directory /home/user/raw_data/ still exists but *does not* contain raw_events_20240315.jsonl (the directory may be empty or contain other files).

These conditions are what the automated grader will assert.
name: task_000000_03f9f146
